clinical_note: A 47-year-old male presented as an urgent transfer from another hospital. He has been experiencing rapid heart rate and sweating. WBC is 21.6 with 86% neutrophils. A band count of 4. ERCP was performed.

clinical_note: The patient presented as an urgent transfer from another hospital. He has been on parenteral nutrition and underwent percutaneous abdominal drainage. label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 52-year-old male presented as an urgent transfer from another hospital. He underwent venous catheterization during his stay. Lab results include a white blood cell count of 16.5, band count 6, and neutrophils 82%.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 63-year-old female patient presented as an urgent transfer from another hospital. She underwent control of epistaxis by anterior nasal packing during her stay. label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: An 81-year-old male presented as an urgent transfer from another hospital. He has been experiencing loss of appetite. During his stay, a white blood cell count of 36.8 was noted with a band count of 10 and neutrophils at 81%. An ERCP with biliary stent was performed.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: Patient presented as an urgent transfer after a week-long stay at another hospital. During his admission, Esophagogastroduodenoscopy with closed biopsy and Colonoscopy were performed. White blood cell count is 7.2, neutrophils 67.9%.

clinical_note: A 34-year-old male presented as an urgent transfer from another hospital. He has been experiencing fever since admission. White blood cell count is 16.2.

clinical_note: The patient presented as an urgent transfer from another hospital. She is a 46-year-old female who has been experiencing cough. label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient presented with chills, malaise, nausea, and sweating. She underwent elective admission after a physician referral. Her white blood cell count is 10.3 with neutrophils 73.2%.
label: Low Infection Concern
